If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.55-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y (Redirects to staging after clicking book travel) **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [More context here](https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/622044#issuecomment-4216397893) ## Action Performed: Precondition: Log in with an account that has Travel Beta enabled and enable Travel in the workspace. Steps to Reproduce: 1. In NewDot, go to Workspaces → Members 2. Invite a new user to the workspace 3. Go to Global Create → Book Travel 4. In the travel web page Navigate to Program → Users within Travel 5. Verify that the newly invited user is created in Expensify Travel 6. Go back to Workspaces → Members 7. Select the added user 8. Click “Remove from workspace” 9. Return to Global Create → Book Travel 10. In the travel web page Navigate to Program → Users within Travel ## Expected Result: The user removed from the workspace should also be removed from Expensify Travel. ## Actual Result: The user is not removed from Expensify Travel after being removed from the workspace. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bba061f9-70ab-4b4b-a4d2-b016861e3624 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] RBR and stale Home to-do persist after deleting a broken card feed

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

labels.name: Hot Pick
labels.description: Ready for an engineer to pick up and run with

body:
## Bug Description After removing a third-party (Plaid) card feed that has a broken connection, stale data remains in Onyx causing two visible issues: 1. **Company cards page shows RBR (Red Brick Road) on the empty state** — the card feed was successfully deleted, but the error indicator persists as if there's still something wrong. 2. **Home page shows a to-do to fix a broken bank connection** — this bank connection no longer exists since the feed was removed, so the to-do is confusing and incorrect. ## Reproduction Steps 1. Have a workspace with a third-party (Plaid) card feed 2. Let the card feed connection break (e.g., the Plaid connection expires and needs re-authentication) 3. As a copilot, remove/delete the card feed entirely 4. Observe the Company cards page — it shows the empty state but still has RBR 5. Observe the Home page — it shows a to-do to fix a broken bank connection that no longer exists ## Expected Behavior After deleting a card feed: - The Company cards page should show a clean empty state with no RBR - The Home page should not show a to-do for a bank connection that was deleted ## Actual Behavior - Company cards empty state still shows RBR - Home page shows a stale to-do to fix a broken bank connection ## Additional Context - The card feed was deleted as a copilot, which may be relevant to the issue - Affected email: jason@expensifail.com - It appears that Onyx is not properly cleaned up when a card feed is removed, leaving behind stale error/connection state **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1775729373571389
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @sobitneupane

title: [$250] Optimize getTransactionsSections and getReportSections

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Coming from https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1775743804085649 ### Background SearchUIUtils.ts powers the Search feature by transforming raw API data into renderable list items. Before processing individual results, the two main functions (getTransactionsSections and getReportSections) need to derive several pieces of metadata -- key classification, column visibility flags, violation maps, and action lookup tables -- each computed by a separate helper that iterates over the entire Object.keys(data). ### Problem The data object is traversed 7+ times by independent helpers (getShouldShowMerchant, buildLastExportedActionByReportIDMap, shouldShowYear, getWideAmountIndicators, getViolations, createReportActionsLookupMaps) before real work begins, and report-level violation checks (hasAnyViolations + hasVisibleViolationsForUser) double-iterate the same violations per report row. This redundant work is a measurable bottleneck on large result sets. ### Solution A new classifyAndPreprocess(data) function replaces all 6 helpers with a single pass that produces every derived structure at once, and a new computeViolationFlags() merges the two violation checks into one loop with early exit. Performance gains | Function                             | Previous Avg (ms) | Current Avg (ms) | Δ (ms) | Improvement | |-------------------------------|----------------------|---------------------|---------|-----------------| | getReportSections            | ~10.9                     | ~8.55                    | -2.35   | ~21.6%          | | getTransactionsSections   | ~0.96                     | ~0.75                    | -0.21   | ~21.9%         | Branch and scenario in the :thread:
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @jmusial

Go to Settings -> Troubleshoot 2. Export Onyx state 3. Open a new browser / incognito window and log into a brand new account 4. Go to Settings -> Troubleshoot 5. If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.52-9 **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Unable to reproduce) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @Justin-Hayes-PH **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1775604154725909) ## Action Performed: 1. Have a workspace with default currency of CAD and a foreign currency default tax rate that differs from the workspace default tax rate 2. Enter an expense with a foreign currency (In this case, USD) ## Expected Result: The foreign currency default tax rate should apply, since it is a foreign currency. ## Actual Result: The expense uses the workspace default tax rate instead of the foreign currency tax rate. NewDot shows that the Workspace Default rate is the "Default rate" while Classic correctly identifies that the default should be the foreign currency default. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshots for the issue are in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1775604154725909) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
